attractions.name: Piedmont Park
attractions.description: 185-acre park in Midtown with walking trails, lake, farmers market, dog park, and skyline views

attractions.name: Atlanta BeltLine
attractions.description: 22-mile multi-use trail loop connecting 45 neighborhoods with parks, public art, restaurants, and retail

attractions.name: High Museum of Art
attractions.description: Leading art museum in the Southeast with collections spanning 15,000+ works from antiquity to present
attractions.frequency: Tue-Sun 10:00-17:00, free second Sunday of each month

attractions.name: Atlanta Botanical Garden
attractions.description: 30-acre garden in Midtown adjacent to Piedmont Park with seasonal exhibits, canopy walk, and orchid collection

attractions.name: Martin Luther King Jr. National Historical Park
attractions.description: Birth home, church, and memorial of Dr. King in the Sweet Auburn historic district
attractions.frequency: Daily 9:00-17:00, free guided tours of birth home

attractions.name: Fox Theatre
attractions.description: Iconic 1929 Moorish-Egyptian revival theater hosting Broadway shows, concerts, and comedy

attractions.name: Ponce City Market
attractions.description: Mixed-use development in former Sears building with food hall, shops, rooftop amusements, and BeltLine access

attractions.name: Kennesaw Mountain National Battlefield Park
attractions.description: 2,965-acre park with Civil War history, 22 miles of hiking trails, and panoramic views of metro Atlanta
attractions.frequency: Daily dawn to dusk, visitor center 9:00-17:00

attractions.name: Decatur Square
attractions.description: Walkable town square with independent restaurants, bookshops, craft breweries, and community events year-round
attractions.frequency: Daily, farmers market Wednesdays, festivals throughout year
